title: 10 best herbal creams for burns

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory agents like aloin, which help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with burns by reducing inflammation.
Wound-healing stimulants: The gel-like substance in aloe vera contains gibberellins, which stimulate collagen synthesis, promote wound contraction, and accelerate the healing process of burned skin.

Antibacterial and antifungal agents: Aloe vera's sulfur-containing compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, have antibacterial and antifungal properties that help prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for burn wounds to heal.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with burns because of its unique properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds that quickly calm and cool the skin, reducing pain and inflammation associated with burns. Additionally, aloe vera has natural antibacterial properties that help prevent infection, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring. This makes aloe vera creams an effective and natural remedy for minor burns and sunburns.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone compound that helps with burns by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, reducing the risk of infection and promoting wound healing.

Aloe barbadensis polysaccharides: Aloe barbadensis polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates that help with burns by stimulating the production of collagen and promoting tissue repair, which accelerates the healing process.

Vitamins C and E: Vitamins C and E are antioxidants that help with burns by neutralizing free radicals and protecting the skin from oxidative damage, which reduces the risk of scarring and promotes wound healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside Aloe vera leaves is widely used due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and promote healing in burn victims.
Leaves (extracted gel): The extracted gel from the leaves contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that aid in the repair of damaged skin tissues and promote faster healing of burns.

Leaves (aloe emodin): The aldehyde compound aloe emodin, found in Aloe vera leaves, has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote the healing process in burns.
remedy_recipe: Gather the following ingredients aloe vera gel 200ml distilled water 50ml beeswax 20g coconut oil 10g vitamin e oil 5ml.
Heat the distilled water in a double boiler until it reaches a temperature of 40c.
Melt the beeswax in the double boiler for 5 minutes then add the coconut oil.
Stir the mixture well then add the aloe vera gel and vitamin e oil and mix until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream contains flavonoids and terpenes that reduce inflammation, which helps to soothe and calm burned skin, reducing redness and swelling.

Antimicrobial: The antioxidants and phenolic acids in pot marigold cream have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection by killing bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause burns to become septic or infected.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold's flavonoids and other compounds promote collagen synthesis, improve blood flow, and enhance the body's natural healing processes, helping to accelerate wound closure and reduce scarring.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, including triterpenoids and carotenoids, promote wound healing by reducing redness, swelling, and pain. Calendula's ability to stimulate collagen production and enhance tissue repair also helps to minimize scarring. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties prevent infection, allowing the burn to heal faster and reducing the risk of complications, making it a valuable topical treatment for burn care.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the risk of infection and promote wound healing in burn injuries.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing inflammation, preventing scarring, and promoting tissue repair in burned areas.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the skin from further damage, reduce oxidative stress, and promote wound healing in burn injuries.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties beneficial for wound healing.
Leaves: The leaves are also used in some creams due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin from further damage.

Stems: Some creams may contain extracts from the stems of Calendula officinalis, which contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that contribute to the plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom time to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve their properties.
Combine 20g of dried flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with minor burns, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing irritated skin.

Antibacterial: Plantain's antimicrobial properties help prevent infection by reducing the growth of bacteria on the burn site, promoting faster healing and minimizing the risk of complications like sepsis.

Wound-healing acceleration: The antioxidants and phytochemicals in plantain creams stimulate collagen production, enhance tissue regeneration, and promote wound closure, accelerating the natural healing process of burns.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Plantago major, such as aucubin and baicalein, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with burns. Additionally, the cream's ability to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and enhancing tissue repair makes it an effective treatment for burn injuries. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective alternative to traditional treatments for burns.
remedy_constituents: Apolysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and protect burned skin by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing pain and promoting tissue repair in burned skin.
Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help reduce inflammation and promote collagen synthesis, contributing to faster wound healing and tissue repair in burned skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain.
Roots: Utilized for their ability to promote cell growth, reduce inflammation, and provide antimicrobial protection, making them suitable for burn treatment.
Stems: Employed for their astringent and antimicrobial properties, which can help in healing and preventing infection in burn wounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried plantago major leaves and flowers to use in the cream recipe.
Infuse 2 cups of water with 1/2 cup of dried plantago major leaves and flowers for 20 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Combine the infused liquid with 1/2 cup of shea butter and the beeswax mixture in a blender.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile creams help reduce inflammation, swelling, and redness associated with burns by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in chamomile cream neutralize free radicals that can cause tissue damage and promote healing by reducing oxidative stress.
Wound-healing promotion property: Chamomile creams have been shown to accelerate wound closure and improve the structure of newly formed skin, potentially leading to faster recovery from burns.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with burns because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with burns. Chamomile's natural cooling effect can provide instant relief, while its antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections. This makes Matricaria chamomilla creams a popular choice for burn care, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with burns by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing swelling and promoting wound healing.

Luteolin: This flavonoid contributes to the burn-healing properties of Matricaria chamomilla by displaying strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help in reducing pain and promoting tissue repair.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene acts as a soothing and anti-inflammatory agent in Matricaria chamomilla creams, helping to reduce redness, itching, and pain associated with burns.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which help soothe and calm burned skin.
Leaves: Their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help reduce pain and promote healing in burned skin.
Seeds: They contain a high amount of essential oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them for 2 hours in a warm place with gentle air circulation.
Infuse 20g of dried flowers in 100ml of carrier oil at 60-70c for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Add 50g of beeswax and 20g of candelilla wax to the oil mixture and heat it at 60-70c for 10-15 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into small glass jars and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection by killing bacteria, viruses, and fungi on the skin, reducing the risk of complications from burn wounds.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and swelling in burned tissue, which helps to minimize tissue damage and promote healing.

Wound-healing enhancement: Lavender's extracts have been shown to enhance wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving skin elasticity, and reducing scarring, making it an effective topical treatment for burns.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The soothing and calming effects of lavender can reduce pain, swelling, and redness associated with burns. The cream's ability to promote wound healing, by increasing blood flow and reducing bacterial growth, aids in the recovery process. Additionally, lavender's calming properties can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it an effective and natural remedy for burns. This can lead to faster healing and reduced scarring.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that helps reduce the risk of infection and promotes wound healing.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress associated with burn wounds.

Caffeic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties that helps protect the skin from further damage, promotes wound healing, and reduces the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect burns.
Leaves: They are used because of their ability to provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic benefits, which aid in wound healing and preventing infection.
Stems: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and promote healing in burn wounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak blooming for optimal potency and fragrance.
Combine 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 200g of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes to facilitate the release of essential oils.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a fragrant infusion.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil with the infusion to create a smooth cream texture.

remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow creams contain soothing anti-inflammatory compounds like mucilage, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by burns.
Protective barrier formation: The gel-like substance in marshmallow creams forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, shielding it from further irritation and damage while promoting healing.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals formed during the burning process, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy tissue regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The mucilage present in the plant forms a protective barrier on the skin, reducing pain and discomfort. It also promotes wound healing by keeping the area moist and preventing infection. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair makes it an effective treatment for minor to moderate burns, allowing for faster recovery and reduced scarring.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help with burns by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing inflammation, and preventing bacterial infections.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids help with burns by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting wound healing through their antioxidant activities.
Galactomannans: Galactomannans help with burns by forming a soothing, protective film on the skin's surface, reducing pain and discomfort, and promoting the healing of damaged tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain high concentrations of mucilage, a natural protective and soothing agent.
Leaves: They also contain mucilage, which helps to calm and protect the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried root of althaea officinalis from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Infuse 200 milliliters of water with 30 grams of dried root in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then mix with 100 grams of sweet almond oil.
Add 50 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat in a double boiler until the wax melts.
Remove from heat and let cool then whip until the mixture thickens to a cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with burns, making it an effective topical treatment for minor burn wounds.

Wound-healing promoter: The allantoin present in comfrey stimulates collagen synthesis, promoting tissue repair, and accelerates wound closure, which is particularly beneficial for burns that may be prone to infection.

Antibacterial and antimicrobial properties: Comfrey's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacterial infections commonly found in burns, reducing the risk of further complications and promoting a faster recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, creams helps with burns because of its rich content of allantoin and other anti-inflammatory compounds. These properties promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, reducing inflammation, and protecting the skin from further damage. Comfrey creams also contain antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and promote tissue regeneration. As a result, applying comfrey cream to burns can aid in the healing process, reducing the risk of scarring and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: It helps with burns by promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and accelerating the removal of dead tissue, thus facilitating the recovery process.

Alkaloids (Symphytum alkaloids, particularly symphytin and symphtine): They help with burns by reducing inflammation, promoting tissue repair, and modulating the immune response to prevent infection and promote healing.
Flavonoids (particularly quercetin and kaempferol): They help with burns by exhibiting antioxidant properties that reduce oxidative stress, prevent tissue damage, and promote the healing process.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain mucilage, a thick and soothing substance that helps to protect and calm burned skin.
Roots: They are rich in mucilage and other compounds that aid in the healing process and provide anti-inflammatory properties to reduce pain and discomfort.
Stems: Similar to the leaves, stems are also a source of mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the skin while it heals from burns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and roots in the early morning for optimal potency.
Combine the harvested plant material in a heat-resistant container and dry it at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered symphytum officinale with 20g of beeswax and 30g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes and then pour it into small containers to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound in goldenseal, has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help prevent infection and reduce redness and swelling in burned areas.
Alkaloids: Goldenseal's alkaloids, such as berbamine and berbamunine, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve pain and discomfort associated with burns.

Antioxidants: Goldenseal contains a variety of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by burns, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of further tissue damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, creams helps with burns because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The berberine content in goldenseal has been shown to accelerate wound healing by promoting the growth of new tissue and reducing inflammation. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help prevent infection and promote a clean environment for the wound to heal. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and protect damaged skin, making it an effective treatment for burns and other skin irritations.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the risk of infection and promote wound healing in burn injuries.
Hydrastine: Hydrastine has antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to prevent infection and promote a clean environment for burn wound healing.
Berbamine: Berbamine has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in burn injuries, promoting wound healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of hydrastine, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: They are rich in hydrastine and berberine, which help reduce pain, inflammation, and promote wound healing.
Rhyzomes: They contain berberine, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in wound healing and infection prevention.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 roots of hydrastis canadensis in late summer or early fall.
Dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 2-3 teaspoons of dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Stir the mixture until it's fully combined and has a smooth consistency then pour into a container.

remedy_properties: Hypotensive (blood-pressure-lowering) effect: St. John's Wort creams contain hypericin, which helps reduce blood flow to the affected area, alleviating pain and reducing the risk of further tissue damage.

Antioxidant properties: The cream's flavonoids and phenolic acids neutralize free radicals, protecting burned skin from oxidative stress and promoting wound healing by reducing inflammation and scarring.

Wound-healing promotion: St. John's Wort contains compounds that stimulate collagen synthesis, promote fibroblast proliferation, and enhance tissue repair, accelerating the natural healing process of burns.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing by increasing blood flow to the affected area. This accelerates the healing process, reduces the risk of infection, and alleviates pain associated with burns, making it a popular natural remedy for burn care. It also helps to reduce scarring and promote tissue regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones: These compounds, particularly hypericin and pseudohypericin, help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Hypericum perforatum, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the skin from oxidative damage and promote tissue repair.

Tannins: Tannins in Hypericum perforatum creams help to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote wound closure by creating a protective barrier on the skin's surface and reducing bacterial growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective for soothing burns.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help in promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation.
Stems: Contain bioflavonoids and phenolic compounds that aid in tissue repair and have antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried st john's wort flowers and leaves from a sunny spot with good air circulation.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours to ensure stability.
Infuse 20g of the dried plant material in 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 10g of the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Emollient properties: Slippery elm cream helps to soothe and calm burned skin by providing a protective barrier that locks in moisture and reduces inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in slippery elm cream can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with burns, promoting faster healing and tissue repair.
Soothing mucilages: Slippery elm's unique mucilage content forms a gel-like coating on the skin that cools and calms burned areas, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, creams helps with burns because it accelerates the healing process by forming a protective barrier on the affected area. The cream's astringent and anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, Ulmus rubra's mucilaginous properties lock in moisture, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and speeding up the recovery time. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to aid in wound healing and alleviate burn-related pain.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Ulmus rubra creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in burns.
Tannins: Tannins in Ulmus rubra creams have astringent properties, which can help reduce fluid loss, prevent infection, and promote wound closure in burns.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, in Ulmus rubra creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress, promote tissue repair, and speed up the healing process in burns.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used to make creams for burns due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: Used to make creams for burns due to its astringent and antiseptic properties that help in wound healing.
Roots: Used to make creams for burns due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of ulmus rubra roots and 50g of ulmus rubra leaves separately in late summer.
Dry the harvested roots and leaves in a warm dark place for 2 weeks.
Grind 50g of dried ulmus rubra roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground root powder with 500ml of distilled water and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes then strain and bottle the resulting herbal cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with burns because they contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce pain, swelling, and inflammation at the site of the burn, promoting a faster healing process. Additionally, ginger's natural antibacterial properties prevent bacterial growth and infection, further enhancing the cream's effectiveness in soothing and repairing burns.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and pain caused by burns.
Antimicrobial properties: Ginger's antimicrobial properties can help prevent infection by reducing bacterial growth on the burn wound, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
Wound-healing promotion: Ginger's ability to stimulate blood flow and promote collagen synthesis helps accelerate the healing process of burns, reducing scarring and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling caused by burns.
Barks: Ginger barks contain salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever that helps soothe burn pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizome roots of zingiber officinale in morning after dew has evaporated weighing 100g.
Dry the harvested ginger roots in shade for 3-4 days or until moisture content reaches 10%.
Grind dried ginger into fine powder using mortar and pestle yielding 50g of powder.
Mix 20% ginger powder with 80% coconut oil in a double boiler at room temperature over low heat stirring occasionally.
Remove mixture from heat and let it cool to 40°c before transferring it to sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with burns because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with burns. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy recovery process and minimizing scarring. As a result, turmeric creams can provide effective relief for minor burns, promoting quick healing and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's main active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation and swelling associated with burns, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
Antioxidant: Curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect damaged skin from further harm by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which can accelerate the healing process.

Wound-healing enhancer: Turmeric has been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis, improve tissue repair, and enhance wound closure rates, promoting healthy scar formation and reducing the appearance of burn scars.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain in burned skin.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain curcuminoids, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties to prevent infection and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality turmeric powder and coconut oil extract with exact measurements of one tablespoon and four ounces respectively.
Combine the ingredients in a clean glass beaker and gently stir for five minutes until well blended and smooth consistency achieved.
Transfer the mixture to a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over simmering water and carefully melt for 10-15 minutes until oil fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and let cool at room temperature for 30-40 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer on low speed until emulsified and lightened in color.
Pour the final cream into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly immediately after preparation for storage in a dark, cool place up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with burns because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, including hamamelitannin and gallic acid, work to reduce redness and swelling, promoting a faster healing process. Witch hazel also has natural astringent properties that help to dry up blisters and prevent infection, while its soothing qualities provide relief from pain and discomfort. As a result, witch hazel creams are often used to treat minor burns, sunburns, and skin irritations, providing gentle and effective relief for affected areas.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The creams contain anti-inflammatory agents like gallic acid and tannins that help reduce swelling and redness associated with burns, allowing for faster healing.

Antibacterial properties: The witch hazel extract has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria like Staphylococcus aureus, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a healthy healing environment.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Witch hazel's astringent and antiseptic properties help to dry out and clean the burn wound, promoting a protective barrier that prevents further irritation and promotes the growth of new tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing by contracting blood vessels and reducing bleeding.

Buds: The buds have a high concentration of witch hazel's active compounds, including gallic acid, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried witch hazel leaves and twigs from the hamamelis virginiana plant.
Grind the dried witch hazel into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 100ml of coconut oil with 20g of grated beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Add 20g of the ground witch hazel powder to the mixing bowl and stir until well combined over low heat.
Remove from heat and let cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping into a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help reduce swelling and pain associated with burn injuries by inhibiting prostaglandin production.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in the creams, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate burn damage and promote tissue repair by protecting against oxidative stress.

Wound-healing promoting properties: Kansas coneflower extracts have been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis, enhance fibroblast proliferation, and increase tensile strength in wounds, all of which contribute to faster and more effective healing of burn injuries.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm burned skin by reducing redness and swelling.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and other nutrients, seeds promote healthy wound healing and tissue regeneration, helping to prevent scarring.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry echinacea angustifolia flowers for 2 weeks in a warm, well-ventilated area.
Combine 20g of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers with 40g of beeswax and 60g of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids after 15 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before whipping and packaging.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with burns because they harness the potent anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea, a natural ingredient derived from the plant. When applied topically to burned skin, these creams work to reduce inflammation and promote healing by soothing redness, itching, and discomfort. Additionally, Kansas coneflower creams' moisturizing benefits help to lock in hydration, accelerating the body's natural repair process and reducing the appearance of scarring. As a result, burns heal faster and with less discomfort when treated with these herbal creams.

Peppermint creams helps with burns because of its natural cooling and soothing properties. The menthol in peppermint oil has a calming effect on the skin, reducing inflammation and pain associated with burns. It also helps to reduce redness and swelling, promoting a faster recovery process. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to prevent infection and promote tissue repair, making it an effective remedy for minor burns and scalds.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with burns by suppressing the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of peppermint cream help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which can worsen burn symptoms and delay healing.

Cooling and soothing: Peppermint's natural cooling effect helps reduce burning pain, itchiness, and discomfort associated with burns, while its soothing properties promote relaxation and calmness, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate burn injuries.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm burn wounds.
Rhyzomes: Containing analgesic and antibacterial properties, rhyzomes of peppermint promote wound healing and reduce pain associated with burns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and dry them properly for 24 hours in a warm area.
Grind 20 grams of dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Melt 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Add the powdered peppermint to the melted coconut oil and stir well for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with burns because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin. The antioxidants present in sage also promote collagen production, reducing the appearance of scars and promoting wound healing. Additionally, the antimicrobial agents in sage cream help prevent infection by reducing bacterial growth, allowing the burn to heal faster and more effectively. Overall, sage creams provide a natural and gentle way to manage burns, reducing discomfort and promoting recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal sage creams contain antimicrobial compounds that help prevent bacterial infection, reducing the risk of sepsis and promoting wound healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and pain associated with burns, making it easier to manage discomfort and promote healing.
Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage creams contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, protecting the skin from further damage caused by oxidative stress and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and reduce pain associated with burns.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, barks have antimicrobial properties that aid in preventing infection and promoting wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the cleaned salvia officinalis leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Combine dried salvia officinalis leaves with 200g of coconut oil in a glass jar and steep for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the herb.
Mix the infused herbal oil with 100g of beeswax and heat until melted at 70°c for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with burns because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacterial infections that can lead to further complications. The cream's active compounds, such as thymol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing by stimulating blood flow and collagen synthesis. Additionally, herbal thyme creams have natural soothing properties, providing immediate relief from pain and discomfort associated with burns, making it a popular remedy for treating minor to moderate burn wounds.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol, inhibit the growth of bacteria on the skin, reducing the risk of infection and promoting faster healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in thyme cream help to reduce inflammation and swelling at the burn site, easing discomfort and promoting tissue repair.
Wound-healing properties: Thyme's compounds stimulate collagen synthesis, improve blood flow, and enhance the body's natural healing processes, leading to faster and more effective wound closure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing inflammation caused by burns.
Barks: Thyme barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce pain and accelerate the healing process of burn wounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them for at least two weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Combine one part dried thymus vulgaris leaves with four parts sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Gently warm the mixture by placing it in a saucepan filled with water or using a double boiler.
Allow the infusion to steep for 30 minutes before straining it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Add one tablespoon of beeswax and stir until melted, then pour the cream into sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with burns because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When applied topically, it soothes the skin by reducing pain, redness, and swelling associated with burns. The cream's antioxidant compounds also help promote wound healing by protecting damaged tissue from further harm. Additionally, rosemary's natural antibacterial agents prevent infection, allowing the burn to heal faster and more effectively, reducing the risk of scarring and promoting a smooth recovery process.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by burn wounds, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with burns, making it easier to manage discomfort.

Wound-healing stimulants: The essential oils in rosemary creams, including camphene and bornyl acetate, have been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis, improve wound contracture, and enhance the healing process by promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing burn inflammation and preventing infection.
Bark: Rosemary bark contains salicylic acid, which helps reduce pain, swelling, and redness associated with burns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them using air circulation method for 2 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 50 grams of distilled water with the ground rosmarinus officinalis powder in a heat-resistant container and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil with the liquid extract and stir until it reaches room temperature for 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with burns because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as inulin and arctiin, work together to reduce inflammation and promote tissue regeneration. This enables faster healing and minimizes the risk of scarring. Additionally, burdock's antimicrobial properties help prevent infection, making it an effective treatment for burns. Overall, herbal burdock creams provide a natural and gentle way to soothe and treat burns, promoting healthy wound recovery and minimizing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with burns by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are pro-inflammatory compounds.

Wound-healing properties: Burdock cream contains antioxidants and flavonoids that promote collagen synthesis, enhance wound contraction, and improve tissue repair, accelerating the healing process for burn wounds.

Antimicrobial properties: Burdock cream's antimicrobial activity helps prevent infection by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, reducing the risk of complications and promoting a healthy healing environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and calm burned skin.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling caused by burns.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arctium lappa roots by digging up mature plants in the fall after foliage has died back.
Dry harvested roots in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Grind dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of powdered arctium lappa with 20 milliliters of melted coconut oil and 5 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for burns are topical treatments made from natural herbs and plant extracts that aid in the healing process of burn wounds. These creams help to reduce inflammation, promote tissue repair, and prevent infection. Examples of herbal creams that help with burns include aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile. They improve lives by providing a natural and non-invasive solution for burn treatment, reducing the risk of scarring and promoting faster healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to burns that are treated with herbal creams are wounds, ulcers, and skin irritations. Herbal creams containing aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and heal damaged skin. These creams can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with these conditions, promoting faster recovery and preventing infection. They are often used to treat minor cuts, scrapes, and abrasions as well.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with burns is Aloe vera, tea tree oil, and calendula. Aloe vera soothes the skin, reduces inflammation, and accelerates the healing process. Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that prevent infection and promote wound healing. Calendula cream enhances the skin's natural barrier function and promotes tissue regeneration. This combination works together to provide a protective barrier, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote fast and effective healing of burns, ultimately promoting a smooth and healthy recovery.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, herbal creams such as Avicenna's Canon of Medicine describing the use of Abu-Khalsa (Arnebia euchroma) ointment (AEO) were found to be effective in healing second-degree burns. The study, which involved 45 patients with second-degree burns, used a single-blind clinical trial method, where one burn wound site of each patient was treated with AEO and the other with silver sulfadiazine cream (SSD), and found that the healing time was significantly shorter in the AEO-treated site (13.9±5.3 days) compared to the SSD-treated site (17.5±6.9 days). This suggests that herbal creams like AEO can be a good option for managing second-degree burns due to their faster healing time and reduced side effects compared to traditional treatments like SSD.
